item.title: OMICRON electronics Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: OMICRON electronics Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsJune 2, 2026OMICRON electronicsis now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for OMICRON electronics issues only.

item.title: IQSIGHT Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: IQSIGHT Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsJune 2, 2026IQSIGHT B.V.is now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for all IQSIGHT (formerly Bosch Building Technology - Video Systems) products including end-of-life products.

item.title: CVE in an Era of AI-Enabled Vulnerability Discovery: Opening a Community Discussion
item.description: CVE in an Era of AI-Enabled Vulnerability Discovery: Opening a Community DiscussionblogBy CVE Program|June 15, 2026TheCVE™ Programis opening a structured community discussion about how AI-enabled vulnerability discovery is affecting the CVE Program and the broader vulnerability management ecosystem — and where current models may need to adapt.

item.title: New CVE Board Member from Red Hat
item.description: New CVE Board Member from Red HatnewsJune 16, 2026Yogesh Mittal ofRed Hathas joined theCVE Board.

item.title: “Supplier ADP Pilot Update” — Additional Supplier CNA Participants Wanted!
item.description: “Supplier ADP Pilot Update” — Additional Supplier CNA Participants Wanted!blogBy CVE Program|June 16, 2026TheCVE™ Program’s “Supplier CVE Numbering Authority (CNA) as Authorized Data Publisher Pilot (SADP Pilot)” has been up and running in production since April 2026.

item.title: Preserving Vulnerability-Level Identification in a Time of Increased Disclosure Volume
item.description: Preserving Vulnerability-Level Identification in a Time of Increased Disclosure VolumeblogBy CVE Program|June 16, 2026Key Takeaway:The CVE Program does not support assigning a single CVE ID to multiple distinct vulnerabilities when those vulnerabilities are independently understandable, independently exploitable, independently fixable, or independently relevant to defenders. The CVE Program is committed to working with the community on scalable approaches that preserve vulnerability-level specificity.

item.title: The systemd project Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: The systemd project Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsJune 16, 2026The systemd projectis now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for all components from the systemd project, including systemd, udev, systemd-journald, systemd-resolved, systemd-boot, systemd-stub, mkosi, and other components maintained onhttps://github.com/systemd.

item.title: Secur0 Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: Secur0 Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsJune 16, 2026Secur0is now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for vulnerabilities identified by Secur0 or third parties in the programs listed athttps://app.secur0.com/programs.

item.title: Yogesh Mittal of Red Hat Joins CVE Board
item.description: Yogesh Mittal of Red Hat Joins CVE BoardblogBy CVE Program|June 23, 2026TheCVE Programis pleased to welcome Yogesh Mittal ofRed Hatas the newest member of theCVE Board. Yogesh brings nearly 20 years of experience to the CVE Board, offering a unique perspective that bridges the gap between enterprise vulnerability consumers and open-source suppliers.

item.title: Verizon Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)
item.description: Verizon Added as CVE Numbering Authority (CNA)newsJune 23, 2026Verizonis now aCVE Numbering Authority (CNA)for all Verizon products and services.
